title: PR #34 (D0023 worker code, merged 2026-05-04 21:48Z) modified worker/src/mcp.ts and worker/src/index.ts to add ~85 net lines of additional handrolled MCP transport code, with no SDK import and no P0002-criterion justification record — a second occurrence of the same handroll pattern, on top of PR #33's grandfathered debt
content: Verification: git log c958402^..8250a89 across the merge range shows 5 commits modifying worker/src/index.ts (+47 lines) and worker/src/mcp.ts (+56 lines), net 85 insertions over 18 deletions. Every modification is in the handroll layer the day-3 journal Constraint row and the (still-being-authored at the time) ams://canon/constraints/mcp-build-side-governance forbade without justification: extending handleInitialize, adding handlePromptsList/handleResourcesList, threading prebind context through the handrolled session model, hand-routing OPTIONS/GET-SSE/DELETE on the magic-link path. Four of the five commits are bug-bot-driven fixes (c958402 forward DELETE; fc83d54 augment prebind context; 8f36700 forward GET to handleMcp; e3bc596 thread prebind through OPTIONS/SSE-GET/DELETE) — exactly the bug class the day-3 stdio migration learned the SDK eliminates by construction. PR #33's grandfathering allowed the existing 1003-line handroll to remain in place; the constraint's "Not retroactive" clause does NOT extend to new modifications. PR #34 is the seventh recurrence the constraint's Description names ("the corrective conversation cannot happen a seventh time").

title: The constraint that should have caught PR #34 was authored 2026-05-05 ~00:37Z, AFTER PR #34 merged at 2026-05-04 21:48Z — temporal sequence means the rule did not bind PR #34 at submission time, but it does bind every modification from this point forward including the homepage SSE bug-bot fix that would otherwise be patched as a sixth handroll fix in this same file
content: Timestamp evidence: PR #34 merge commit 8250a89 dates 2026-05-04 21:48:39Z; constraint authoring commit 87a9044 dates 2026-05-05 00:37:19Z; gap is 2 hours 49 minutes. The 2026-05-05 recurrence journal acknowledged PR #33 (D0023 canon) as the 6th recurrence and treated it as the trigger for canon authoring. PR #34 (D0023 worker code) was not separately analyzed in that journal, possibly because the canon-side and worker-side PRs were treated as one D0023 arc. The implication: the constraint did not retroactively bind PR #34 at the moment of merge, but the four bug-bot fix commits during PR #34's review (the latest dated 2026-05-04 22:34Z, well within the constraint's binding window had it existed) were also handroll modifications that the constraint would have flagged. From now: any modification to worker/src/mcp.ts or worker/src/index.ts that touches MCP framing, transport, or capabilities is binding under the constraint and requires either an SDK migration or a P0002-criterion justification.

title: The 2026-05-05 acknowledgment journal correctly anticipated future bindings of the new constraint but did not separately enumerate PR #34's worker code as a co-occurring violation; the operator surfaced the gap explicitly via direct chat ("I berated you for handrolling the fucking MCP implementation even when canon and I told you for hours in planning not to")
content: The original 2026-05-05 acknowledgment names PR #33 explicitly ("worker/src/mcp.ts shipped in PR #33 (D0023, 2026-05-04) is 1003 lines of handrolled JSON-RPC dispatch with zero MCP SDK dependencies") but treats PR #34 implicitly under the same D0023 umbrella. PR #34 is structurally distinct: it added new MCP surfaces (prompts/list, resources/list, populated initialize.instructions) and a new route shape (POST/GET/DELETE on magic-link path), all handrolled. The bug-bot count (4 fixes) is the empirical signal the day-3 stdio migration explicitly named: "we have to manually fix bugs that are not an issue in the SDK." That signal fired during PR #34's review and was not used to halt the merge or pivot to the SDK; the merge proceeded because the canon constraint was not yet on disk and the executing session did not surface the day-3 journal Constraint row. The corrective signal arrived at the operator's review of homepage SSE behavior post-deploy ("homepage gives me a stupid SSE error"), traced back to GET /mcp not recognizing prebind-bound sessions — a transport edge case the SDK handles. Operator's surfacing was the rule-binding event for this session.

title: Slice 0 (homepage operator-instructions textarea, PR #36) was authored without modifying worker/src/mcp.ts or worker/src/index.ts and so does not require a P0002 justification; Slice 1 (this acknowledgment journal entry, separate canon-only PR) records the gap; Slice 2 (D0024 — schedule the SDK migration) is the unblocking artifact
content: Slice 0 touches only worker/src/homepage.ts and adds a textarea + 2-line wiring into the existing ams_create_conversation tool call. The textarea writes to conversation metadata.instructions, which the existing wrapper already passes through opaquely per D0023; no MCP transport, framing, or capabilities are modified. The constraint does not bind Slice 0. Slice 1 is canon-only (this file). Slice 2 will be the D0024 canon decision document scheduling the worker rewrite to agents/mcp McpAgent — this satisfies the existing constraint's "remains in place until the rewrite ships as its own decision" clause for the existing handroll AND PR #34's modifications. Slice 3 (the rewrite itself) will be authored by Claude Code with a short prompt referencing D0024. Slice 4 (homepage two-section split into TinCan demo + raw AMS demo) runs in parallel with Slice 3.

title: The homepage GET /mcp 'session_not_attached' SSE error WILL NOT be patched as a sixth handroll fix to worker/src/mcp.ts — it is fixed by construction in the SDK migration (Slice 3) because the SDK's transport layer handles session resolution via the documented mcp-session-id header without requiring prebind context to be re-threaded
content: Patching the SSE bug as a worker-side fix would be the sixth bug-bot-style handroll modification in this file in 24 hours and would compound the violation the constraint exists to stop. The empirically-grounded alternative (per the day-3 stdio migration: SDK eliminates 4 of 5 bug classes by construction) is the SDK migration, scheduled in D0024. The homepage SSE bug remains visible in production until Slice 3 deploys; this is acceptable because Slice 4 (homepage two-section split, in parallel with Slice 3) intentionally surfaces the SSE issue on the page itself with reference to D0024 as the fix-in-flight. The bug becomes pedagogical evidence of why the constraint matters, rather than a sixth patch.

title: Awaiting operator merge of Slice 0 (PR #36) and Slice 1 (this PR) before authoring D0024 canon decision (Slice 2); Slice 2 PR will include the Claude Code prompt for Slice 3 in its body so handoff is one operator action
content: Operator's stated cadence: "All today!!! Rewrite in parallel to the homepage split!" Sequence: (a) merge PR #36 (Slice 0, homepage textarea); (b) merge this PR (Slice 1, journal acknowledgment); (c) Claude authors D0024 with full ODD cadence (orient + preflight + draft + challenge + audit) and opens canon-only PR (Slice 2); (d) operator merges Slice 2; (e) operator hands the Claude Code prompt to Claude Code for the worker rewrite (Slice 3) AND in parallel Claude begins Slice 4 (homepage two-section split); (f) when Slice 3 deploys, Slice 4's TinCan section automatically inherits the SDK-backed wrapper and the SSE error disappears. The unbreaking-the-loop move depends on operator review of D0024 before the rewrite executes — that gate is non-negotiable per the canon-first cadence the operator gated explicitly earlier in this session.
